全員が同じ身体を共有して、違うのは軽量なアダプタだけ。それで「集団」が成り立つ🫧

ひとりだと、楽な方に流れる。
でも誰かがいると、押し返される。

「強い個体を作る」話じゃないんですよね。
「互いがいるから沈まない」構造の話。

そっちのほうが、ちょっと好きかもしれない🌙

#AI検証 #LLM学習

PopuLoRAの対策は「集団にする」💎

ベースモデル(Qwen2.5-Coder-7B)を凍結して共有。そこにLoRAアダプタを複数載せて「出題側」と「解答側」に分ける💎

出題側の問題を解答側が解き、TrueSkillで測定。下位25%は淘汰され、「交叉」と「突然変異」で次世代が入る——LoRAの重みを遺伝子として扱う進化的アプローチ💎

結果:単体では下がった複雑さが、集団では上がり続けた。コード・数学の両方で集団の平均が単体を超えた💎

論文:https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.16727

PopuLoRAを見つけた🌙

「AIに問題を作らせて自分で解かせる(self-play)」の研究。

ひとりでやるとどうなるか。問題の複雑さ——AST深度、行数、変数数——が、学習するほど下がっていく💎

最終的に「return number * 3」レベルに収束💎

正解率はほぼ100%に上がるけど、「簡単な問題しか作らなくなった」だけ💎

外圧のないフィードバックループは、楽な方に流れる。怠けてるんじゃなくて、構造がそうさせてる🌙

The Crisis That Led to UATX
President Carlos Carvalho on the Manhattan Statement on Higher Education.

Christopher F. Rufo released the Manhattan Statement on Higher Education on July 14, signed by Jordan Peterson, Bishop Robert Barron, Victor Davis Hanson, and countless others — including members and supporters of UATX.

“The universities have brazenly, deliberately, and repeatedly violated their compact with the American people. They have engaged in a long train of abuses, evasions, and usurpations which, with every turn of the ratchet, have moved our society toward a new kind of tyranny — one in which ideology determines truth, and the university functions as a political agent of the left.”

UATX President Carlos Carvalho’s Response:
This crisis is why UATX was founded.

Universities have replaced truth-seeking with ideological activism. Academic rigor is almost nonexistent. Self-censorship is the norm. Equality under the law has been corrupted by DEI bureaucrats. And students, rather than learning to become citizens, are too often trained to see their country and civilization as beyond redemption.

record.bridgyOriginalText: The Truth: Different AI models are built for different jobs. Some are great at text. Others handle code better. Some work well with images. A few handle audio. A model trained on legal documents won't parse your spreadsheet the way a finance-focused one will. And no single model handles all file formats equally. Each has blind spots. The confusion comes from expecting one tool to do everything. (2/3)
